title: Allow late video frames to drop under load
summary: 0.6.18 keeps the 0.6.17 retirement of active once-per-second hard AV correction, but changes the Unity VideoPlayer posture so it can drop late frames instead of clinging to backlog and turning stress into visible stutter, catch-up jumps, and audio drift.
changes: Change the standalone hosted-player VideoPlayer setup to use skipOnDrop = true.
Keep the current seek, focus, resize, preset, and plugin UI behavior unchanged.
Stamp version 0.6.18 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs.
Build the 0.6.18 player runtime and release outputs cleanly.
Refresh the raw dev_deploy baseline player and modern_tv hosted proof artifacts.
reasoning: The current playback issue pattern points more strongly at decode backlog pressure than at the retired periodic hard-correction seam.
When video falls behind, allowing the player to drop late frames is safer than trying to preserve every frame and then visibly catching up in bursts.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: The next raw alpha should feel less prone to periodic catch-up stalls and fast-forward behavior on stressed video playback.
This slice does not add a new sync algorithm; it strictly changes the recovery posture when video falls behind.
knownIssues: If stutter remains after 0.6.18, the next likely seam is the clocking model itself rather than the retired periodic correction or frame-drop posture.
Multiple active instances can still increase decode pressure and should remain part of manual testing.
